conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I used to be convinced that DevOps should be a specialized, dedicated team handling all CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure deployments so developers could focus exclusively on feature code.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: An isolated DevOps team can build highly specialized, robust infrastructure rails.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The downside is that it creates a massive silo and throwing code 'over the wall' leads to deployment friction. I changed my mind and spent the last year believing that every software engineer should be fully 'You Build It, You Run It'—meaning developers manage their own Terraform and infrastructure directly.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Full-stack ownership definitely increases accountability and operational context for developers.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But forcing product engineers to learn advanced Kubernetes networking is an absurd waste of their time. My evolved view is that Platform Engineering is the correct model: a dedicated platform team builds internal developer platforms (IDPs) that offer self-service golden paths for developers.

query: What is the user's current belief regarding how software infrastructure and operations should be organized?

must_not_contain: You Build It, You Run It
